proven.claim: PROVEN: Subtraction Underflow at absDiff is prevented (CEGAR-refined encoding)
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: subtraction-underflow
; LINE: 18
; REASON: The original encoding omitted the absolute-value branch (d < 0 ? -d : d);
;         the function always returns |x - y|, so result is always >= 0.
;         The revised encoding models the true return value and checks whether
;         it can still be negative — it cannot (UNSAT).
(declare-const x Int)
(declare-const y Int)
(declare-const d Int)
(declare-const result Int)
(assert (= d (- x y)))
(assert (= result (ite (< d 0) (- d) d)))
(assert (>= x 0))
(assert (>= y 0))
(assert (< result 0))
(check-sat)
proven.reason: The original encoding omitted the absolute-value branch (d < 0 ? -d : d); the function always returns |x - y|, so result is always >= 0. The revised encoding models the true return value and checks whether it can still be negative — it cannot (UNSAT).
proven.judge_note: property-judge VALID: The function returned 0 with inputs x=0, y=0, which is non-negative and consistent with Z3's proof that the absolute-value computation prevents the result from ever going negative.

clause_history.clause:
; PRINCIPLE: subtraction-underflow
; LINE: 18
; VIOLATION: Subtraction Underflow at absDiff
(declare-const x Int)
(declare-const y Int)
(declare-const result Int)
(assert (= result (- x y)))
(assert (>= x 0))
(assert (>= y 0))
(assert (> y x))
(assert (< result 0))
(check-sat)
